title: Command safety and help deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Command safety and help; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Command safety and help: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A familiar delete command is safe to run immediately if the typed path looks approximately correct.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Verify resolved scope and current state, prefer non-destructive previews and exact-path APIs, and keep a recovery path before destructive execution.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Verify resolved scope and current state, prefer non-destructive previews and exact-path APIs, and keep a recovery path before destructive execution.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Command safety and help instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Verify resolved scope and current state, prefer non-destructive previews and exact-path APIs, and keep a recovery path before destructive execution.
A familiar delete command is safe to run immediately if the typed path looks approximately correct.
